ai_strategy.summary: Loyola University Chicago has a public AI posture anchored in Generative AI Feature Catalog. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Loyola University Chicago publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: Generative AI Feature Catalog
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Loyola University Chicago is evidenced through Generative AI Feature Catalog and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot and AI services catalog
initiatives.description: Loyola ITS maintains a public AI services catalog that includes Microsoft Copilot, Adobe AI tools, Zoom AI Companion, Gradescope, Respondus, Panopto, and other AI-enabled teaching or enterprise tools.
initiatives.significance: The catalog closes a platform-identification gap by naming institution-supported AI tooling instead of only generic guidance.

investment.description: Public catalog does not disclose enterprise contract values or active-user counts.

collaborations.description: Vendors are listed in Loyola ITS AI services guidance.

initiatives.policy_implications: Routes AI use through approved service categories and ITS guidance.

initiatives.title: NSF broadened-participation computing research access award
initiatives.description: Loyola reports a $327,690 NSF award for expanding access to computing research among women at schools with smaller computing programs, providing a public student-subgroup impact signal tied to computing and AI/ML pathways.
initiatives.significance: The award connects AI-adjacent computing research capacity with an explicit underrepresented-student access goal.

investment.description: NSF award reported by Loyola awards-and-contracts archive.

collaborations.description: Federal grant support for broadened participation in computing research.

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: Loyola University Chicago has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is Generative AI Feature Catalog, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
Some AI initiatives did not disclose dollar amounts, user counts, or detailed implementation metrics.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
Loyola publishes supported AI tools and GPU-server availability, but public pages do not disclose active-user counts, Microsoft Copilot contract value, or GPU-card totals.
No campus-wide AI student-success outcome metric was found beyond broadened-participation grant purpose statements.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: platform first.
